THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING WITH A MODIFICATION AS TO THE ISSUE OF SANCTIONS, AS FOLLOWS: MOTION DENIED AS MOOT. SANCTIONS GRANTED IN FAVOR OF THE CITY IN THE AMOUNT OF $500.00 AGAINST THE PARTY PLAINTIFF (NOT PLAINTIFF'S ATTORNEY). ALL OTHER SANCTIONS, IF ANY, ARE DENIED (SEE WORD MINUTES FOR COMPLETE RULING.) THE CITY SHALL PREPARE A FORM OF THE ORDER RECAPITULATING THE COURT'S RULING, TIMELY COMPLY WITH CAL. R. CT. 3.1312, AND SUBMIT THE PROPOSED ORDER TO THE JUDGE PRO TEM AT DROBBINS@TONGROBBINS.COM. DISCOVERY, DEPT. 302, JUL-24-2020 HEARING HELD RE: DEFENDANTS FULL BRIGHT PROPERTIES, LLC AND CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER DISCOVERY RESPONSES. THE COURT MODIFIES ITS TENTATIVE RULING AND RULES AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANTS' MOTION IS GRANTED. SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES: DEFENDANTS HAVE NARROWED THE SCOPE OF THE INTERROGATORIES 75 AND 76 TO THE HEAD, NECK, BACK, ARMS AND LEGS, THE AREAS OF THE BODY PLAINTIFF ALLEGES WERE INJURED IN THE SUBJECT ACCIDENT. PLAINTIFF IS TO PROVIDE CLEAR, FURTHER RESPONSES IN COMPLIANCE WITH CCP 2030.220 IDENTIFYING PROVIDERS AND HOSPITALS TREATING LIMITED TO THESE AREAS OF THE BODY. REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION: PLAINTIFF IS PROVIDE A CLEAR, FURTHER RESPONSE TO REQUEST NO. 8 IN COMPLIANCE WITH CCP 2031.230 LIMITED TO THE AREAS OF THE BODY IN ISSUE AND SHALL PRODUCE RESPONSIVE NON-PRIVILEGED DOCUMENTS. CLEAR, FURTHER RESPONSES AND DOCUMENTS ARE TO BE SERVED WITHIN 20-DAYS OF THE ORDER. PLAINTIFF'S OBJECTIONS ARE NOT DEEMED WAIVED. SANCTIONS IN THE AMOUNT OF $750 ARE TO BE PAID WITHIN 10-DAYS OF THE ORDER. DEFENSE COUNSEL IS TO PREPARE AND SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER TO THE JUDGE PRO TEM. JUDGE PRO TEM: STEPHANIE KRMPOTIC; CLERK: SEAN KANE; NOT REPORTED. (302/JPT)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Discovery Responses; And For Monetary Sanctions In The Amount Of 2,234.95
Off Calendar Aug-30-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. BENJAMIN G. BERKLEY, Esq. of Carpenter, Zuckerman & Rowley, LLP (Tel: 310-273-1230) for plaintiff Mario Salvador Ruiz Ruano;
Atty. CAITLIN M. PHAIR, Esq. of O'Brien Law, P.C. (Tel: 707-789-6500) for defendant City and County of San Francisco.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO's Motion To Compel Further Discovery Responses; And For Monetary Sanctions In The Amount Of $2,234.95.

Both sides stipulate to the matter being decided by the Judge Pro Tem. The Judge Pro Tem and counsel for both sides (all appearing remotely) authorize the clerk to sign the formal stipulate form on their behalf.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling with a modification as to the issue of sanctions, as follows:

Defendant City and County of San Francisco's ("City's") Motion to Compel Further Discovery Responses is DENIED AS MOOT. Sanctions are granted in favor of the City in the amount of $500.00 against the party plaintiff (not plaintiff's attorney). All other sanctions, if any, are DENIED

(See Word minutes for complete ruling.)

The City shall prepare a form of the order recapitulating the Court's ruling, timely comply with Cal. R. Ct. 3.1312, and submit the proposed order to the Judge Pro Tem at drobbins@tongrobbins.com.

Judge Pro Tem: Douglas Robbins; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/JPT)

Off Calendar Jul-13-2021 Text Ruling

MANDATORY SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Plaintiffs Compliance With Demand For Production Or In The Alternative For Further Responses And For Monetary Sanctions In The Amount Of 2,526.00
Off Calendar Jul-02-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO's Motion To Compel Plaintiffs Compliance With Demand For Production Or In The Alternative For Further Responses And For Monetary Sanctions In The Amount Of 2,526.00:

Motion granted. The Court recognizes that Plaintiff has had difficulty locating his financial records, however, trial is scheduled for July 19, 2021. As to RFPD No. 1, Plaintiff shall provide a supplemental response and produce all checking and saving bank statements from 2015 - present by July 13, 2021. If Plaintiff has no documents, then a response to Interrogatory No. 5 will be required. The deadline will be extended by 30 days if the Presiding Judge grants a motion to continue the trial date. The request for sanctions is denied.

Moving party to submit a proposed order that is verbatim with the Court's ruling via email to davididaho14@gmail.com.

Judge Pro Tem: David McDonald, Clerk: S. Le, Not reported. =(302/JPT)

MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Nov-10-2020 Continued To Jul-19-21 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

MOTION TO CONTINUE COURT TRIAL
Off Calendar Nov-10-2020 Text Ruling

MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Jun-15-2020 Continued To Feb-22-21 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Notice Of Motion To Compel Further Discovery Responses
Off Calendar Jul-24-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE:
Atty. BENJAMIN BERKELEY, Esq. of Carpenter, Zuckerman & Rowley, LLP (Tel: 310-273-1230) for Plaintiff Mario Salvador Ruiz Ruano;
Atty. JESSICA TAYLOR, Esq. of O'Brien Law, P.C. (Tel: 707-789-6500) for Defendants Full Bright Properties, LLC, County of San Francisco and City of San Francisco.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANTS FULL BRIGHT PROPERTIES, LLC AND CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S Motion To Compel Further Discovery Responses.

The parties stipulate to the matter being decided by the judge pro tem. The parties and the judge pro tem authorize the clerk to sign the stipulation form on their behalf.

The matter is argued and submitted. The Court modifies its tentative ruling and rules as follows: Defendants' Motion is granted. Special Interrogatories: Defendants have narrowed the scope of the interrogatories 75 and 76 to the head, neck, back, arms and legs, the areas of the body plaintiff alleges were injured in the subject accident. Plaintiff is to provide clear, further responses in compliance with CCP 2030.220 identifying providers and hospitals treating limited to these areas of the body. Request for Production: Plaintiff is provide a clear, further response to Request No. 8 in compliance with CCP 2031.230 limited to the areas of the body in issue and shall produce responsive non-privileged documents. Clear, further responses and documents are to be served within 20-days of the order. Plaintiff's objections are not deemed waived. Sanctions in the amount of $750 are to be paid within 10-days of the order.

Defense counsel is to prepare and submit a proposed order to the judge pro tem.

Judge Pro Tem: Stephanie Krmpotic; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/JPT)
